features.summary: Walmart reported strong Q2 fiscal 2022 results with revenue growth of 7.6% in constant currency excluding divestitures and operating income growth of 24.1%. Walmart U.S. comp sales grew 5.2% with strong grocery market share gains, while eCommerce grew 6% on top of 103% two-year stack growth. The company is successfully executing its omnichannel strategy, serving customers across stores, pickup, and delivery channels. New revenue streams are scaling rapidly, including Walmart Connect advertising business which nearly doubled, and marketplace fulfillment services approaching double-digit GMV penetration.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year guidance significantly, now expecting consolidated net sales growth of 6-7% and adjusted operating income growth of 11.5-14% versus prior high single-digit guidance. Adjusted EPS guidance increased to $6.20-$6.35 range. The company expects to reach $75 billion in global eCommerce sales this year, progressing toward $100 billion near-term. Management emphasized continued investment in supply chain automation and capacity while maintaining confidence in long-term growth algorithm of 4% sales growth with profit growing faster than sales.
features.risks: The company faces ongoing supply chain disruptions requiring vessel chartering and extended lead times to secure inventory for holiday seasons. COVID-related uncertainties persist with the Delta variant potentially affecting shopping patterns and operations. Inflationary pressures are emerging across categories, though management believes they can maintain competitive pricing through various operational levers. International markets continue facing COVID-related government restrictions that could impact performance.
features.key_drivers: Strong grocery performance driven by improved fresh presentation, supply chain execution, and market share gains with units growing faster than dollar share. Rapid scaling of higher-margin businesses including advertising (Walmart Connect), marketplace services, and fulfillment operations creating new profit streams. Successful omnichannel integration allowing customers to shop seamlessly across channels, with technology innovations like Scan & Go and cloud-powered checkout being deployed globally. Continued digital transformation and automation investments positioning the company for long-term competitive advantages.
